name: vedro-unittest

metadata.name: vedro-unittest
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Allows running unittest test cases within the Vedro framework

documentation:
# Vedro Unittest [![Codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/vedro-universe/vedro-unittest/main.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/vedro-universe/vedro-unittest) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/vedro-unittest.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-unittest/) [![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/vedro-unittest?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-unittest/) [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/vedro-unittest.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/vedro-unittest/) [vedro-unittest](https://pypi.org/project/vedro-unittest/) allows running unittest test cases within the Vedro framework. This plugin seamlessly integrates unittest test cases, converting them into Vedro scenarios to leverage the powerful features of the Vedro testing framework. ## Installation
Quick

For a quick installation, you can use a plugin manager as follows: ```shell $ vedro plugin install vedro-unittest ```

Manual

To install manually, follow these steps: 1. Install the package using pip: ```shell $ pip3 install vedro-unittest ``` 2. Next, activate the plugin in your `vedro.cfg.py` configuration file: ```python # ./vedro.cfg.py import vedro import vedro_unittest class Config(vedro.Config): class Plugins(vedro.Config.Plugins): class VedroUnitTest(vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTest): enabled = True ```

## Usage To use the plugin, create your unittest test cases as usual: ```python # ./scenarios/test_base64.py import unittest from base64 import b64encode, b64decode class TestBase64Encoding(unittest.TestCase): def test_encode_banana_to_base64(self): result = b64encode(b"banana") self.assertEqual(result, b"YmFuYW5h") def test_decode_banana_from_base64(self): result = b64decode(b"YmFuYW5h") self.assertEqual(result, b"banana") ``` Then run your tests using Vedro: ```shell $ vedro run ``` This will automatically detect and run your unittest test cases as Vedro scenarios, allowing you to take advantage of Vedro's rich feature set.

classes.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the VedroUnitTestPlugin.

```
param config

The configuration class for the plugin.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribe to Vedro events for handling unittest scenarios.

```
param dispatcher

The event dispatcher to register listeners on.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.on_config_loaded
documentation:
Handle the configuration loaded event to register the unittest scenario loader.

```
param event

The configuration loaded event containing the application config.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.on_scenario_passed
documentation:
Handle a scenario passed event to capture expected failure details.

```
param event

The scenario passed event containing scenario results.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.on_scenario_failed
documentation:
Handle a scenario failed event to capture unexpected success details.

```
param event

The scenario failed event containing scenario results.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin.on_exception_raised
documentation:
Handle an exception raised event to filter traceback information.

```
param event

The exception raised event containing exception details.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin._get_expected_failure
documentation:
Retrieve the expected failure exception from a scenario.

```
param scenario

The scenario to inspect.

return

The expected failure exception, or None if not applicable.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTestPlugin._get_unexpected_success
documentation:
Retrieve the unexpected success exception from a scenario.

```
param scenario

The scenario to inspect.

return

The unexpected success exception, or None if not applicable.
```

A plugin for integrating unittest test cases into the Vedro framework.

Provides support for running unittest-based tests as Vedro scenarios, capturing
expected failures, unexpected successes, and filtering traceback information.

classes.name: vedro_unittest._vedro_unittest.VedroUnitTest
documentation: Configuration class for the VedroUnitTestPlugin.

classes.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.UnexpectedSuccessError
documentation:
Represents an unexpected success in a test scenario.

Used to indicate that a test case passed when it was expected to fail.

classes.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.__init__
documentation: Initialize the TestResult instance with custom tracking lists.

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addSuccess
documentation:
Record the success of a test case.

```
param test

The test case that succeeded.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addError
documentation:
Record an error that occurred during the execution of a test case.

```
param test

The test case that caused the error.

param err

The exception information for the error.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addFailure
documentation:
Record a failure for a test case.

```
param test

The test case that failed.

param err

The exception information for the failure.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addSkip
documentation:
Record that a test case was skipped.

```
param test

The test case that was skipped.

param reason

The reason for skipping the test case.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addSubTest
documentation:
Record the result of a subtest.

```
param test

The parent test case.

param subtest

The subtest case being reported.

param err

The exception information, if the subtest failed or had an error.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addExpectedFailure
documentation:
Record a test case that failed as expected.

```
param test

The test case that was expected to fail.

param err

The exception information for the expected failure.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult.addUnexpectedSuccess
documentation:
Record a test case that unexpectedly succeeded.

```
param test

The test case that unexpectedly succeeded.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_result.TestResult._get_exception
documentation:
Extract the exception instance from exception information.

```
param err

The exception information tuple.

return

The exception instance.
```

Extends unittest.TestResult to handle custom test result reporting.

Tracks exceptions, expected failures, and unexpected successes during
the execution of unit tests.

classes.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the UnitTestLoader with a module loader.

```
param module_loader

The loader responsible for loading Python modules.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader.load
documentation:
Load scenarios from a Python module located at the specified path.

```
param path

The path to the Python module to load.

return

A list of Vedro-compatible scenarios.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._collect_scenarios
documentation:
Collect test scenarios from a Python module.

```
param module

The loaded Python module.

return

A list of Vedro-compatible scenarios.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._extract_tests_from_suite
documentation:
Extract individual test cases from a test suite.

```
param test_suite

The test suite or test case to extract from.

return

A list of test cases.

raises TypeError

If an unsupported test type is encountered.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._get_first_test
documentation:
Get the first test case from a test suite.

```
param test_suite

The test suite or test case to examine.

return

The first test case found.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._build_vedro_scenario
documentation:
Build a Vedro scenario from a unittest test or test suite.

```
param test

The test or test suite to wrap in a scenario.

param path

The file path of the test.

param name

The name of the scenario.

param subject

The subject description of the scenario.

return

A Vedro-compatible scenario type.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._run_test
documentation:
Run a unittest test or test suite and capture its results.

```
param test

The test or test suite to run.

return

The results of running the test.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._process_test_result
documentation:
Process the results of a test run and apply them to the scenario.

```
param scenario

The scenario type associated with the test.

param test_result

The results of the test run.

raises ExceptionGroup

If multiple exceptions are grouped (Python 3.11+).

raises BaseException

If a single exception occurred.

raises UnexpectedSuccessError

If a test unexpectedly succeeded.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._has_module_setup_or_teardown
documentation:
Check if a module has setup or teardown methods.

```
param module

The module to check.

return

True if the module has setup or teardown methods, otherwise False.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._has_class_setup_or_teardown
documentation:
Check if a test case's class has setup or teardown methods.

```
param test_case

The test case to check.

return

True if the class has setup or teardown methods, otherwise False.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._is_method_overridden
documentation:
Determine if a method is overridden in a subclass.

```
param method_name

The name of the method to check.

param child_class

The subclass to examine.

param parent_class

The parent class to compare against.

return

True if the method is overridden, otherwise False.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._is_test_skipped
documentation:
Check if a test or test suite is marked as skipped.

```
param test_suite

The test or test suite to check.

return

A tuple indicating if the test is skipped and the reason.
```

methods.name: vedro_unittest._test_loader.UnitTestLoader._get_test_attr
documentation:
Retrieve an attribute from a test case or its method.

```
param test

The test case to query.

param name

The name of the attribute to retrieve.

param default

The default value to return if the attribute is not found.

return

The value of the attribute, or the default value.
```

Loads unit test scenarios and converts them into Vedro-compatible scenarios.

Handles the discovery and conversion of unittest-based tests within the Vedro framework.
